title: JEP 2026 – Call for Applications
summary: Applications are now open for JEP 2026 , a program designed for scholars advancing research in education policy with a focus on racial justice. About the Program JEP (Junior Education Policy) brings together emerging researchers in a collaborative learning environment to strengthen their research agendas and impact. Participants will: Engage in community-based learning with peers Refine and develop their research focus Receive mentorship and guidance from experienced scholars Explore how research can drive equity and systemic change Who Should Apply This opportunity is ideal for: Current doctoral students in education policy Postdoctoral researchers working on education and racial justice Scholars interested in equity-focused research and policy impact Applicants should demonstrate: A strong interest in racial justice in education Commitment to advancing impactful research Willingness to engage in collaborative learning Key Details Program: JEP 2026 Eligibility: Doctoral students and post-docs in education policy Focus Area: Racial justice in education Application Deadline: June 1, 2026 Why This Opportunity Matters JEP provides a supportive space for emerging scholars to build meaningful, equity-driven research while connecting with a community committed to transforming education systems. How to Apply Apply here. For More Opportunities, Visit OFY here. The post JEP 2026 – Call for Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: SRHR Ambassadors 2026 Cohort – Call for Applications
summary: The Jonica Foundation is recruiting County SRHR Ambassadors for its 2026 cohort. This opportunity is designed for young women who are ready to lead conversations and drive change in sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR) within their communities. About the Program The SRHR Ambassadors initiative empowers young women to become advocates and educators on critical health and social issues affecting women and girls. Ambassadors will: Lead community conversations on SRHR topics Raise awareness and educate peers Advocate for better health outcomes Engage in leadership and personal development Focus Areas Participants will work on key topics such as: Menstrual health Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) Gender-Based Violence (GBV) Contraception awareness Menopause education Who Should Apply This program is open to: Young women in universities and colleges Residents of Nairobi, Kiambu, Narok, Homa Bay, and Kilifi (Kenya) Individuals passionate about advocacy, health education, and community impact Key Details Program: SRHR Ambassadors 2026 Cohort Organization: Jonica Foundation Location: Kenya (selected counties) Application Deadline: 15 May 2026 Why This Opportunity Matters SRHR issues directly affect the well-being, rights, and opportunities of women and girls. This program equips young women with the knowledge, voice, and leadership skills to drive meaningful change in their communities . How to Apply Apply here. title: Move to Estonia Without IELTS 2026/2027 – Scholarships
summary: Looking to study or work in Europe without IELTS? Estonia offers a range of scholarships and grants for international students, researchers, and academic staff. These opportunities are coordinated by the Estonian Ministry of Education and Research and the Education and Youth Board (Harno) , aiming to attract global talent and promote academic collaboration. About the Opportunity Estonia provides multiple funding options under the Estonian Scholarship Programme, supporting: Degree studies (Bachelor’s, Master’s, PhD) Short-term exchange programs Summer and winter schools Research and academic collaboration These programs are designed to strengthen international cooperation and support innovation in education and research. Can You Study Without IELTS? In many cases, yes. Some Estonian universities allow alternatives such as: Proof that your previous education was in English Online interviews or internal language assessments However, requirements vary by university and program, so always confirm before applying. Types of Scholarships Available 1. Government Scholarships Funded by Estonia to support international students across different levels of study. 2. Scholarships for Students Support for degree and exchange studies, especially at Master’s and PhD levels. 3. Grants for Researchers Academic Staff For teaching, research collaboration, and academic exchange at Estonian universities. 4. Summer Short-Term Programmes Funding to attend short courses and academic programs in Estonia. What the Funding Covers Depending on the program, support may include: Tuition fees (full or partial) Monthly stipend Travel or accommodation support Participation fees for courses Who Can Apply Eligible applicants include: International students (Bachelor’s, Master’s, PhD) Researchers and academic staff Individuals interested in short-term academic programs Key Details Location: Estonia Eligible Levels: Bachelor’s, Master’s, PhD, Research Application Deadline: Around May 2026 (varies by program; some close May 14, 2026) Why This Opportunity Matters Estonia is known for its strong digital economy and innovative education system. These scholarships offer a pathway to study, research, and build international experience in Europe , often with flexible language requirements. How to Apply Learn more and apply here. For more Scholarship opportunities, Visit OFY here. The post Move to Estonia Without IELTS 2026/2027 – Scholarships appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: NASA Nebraska Space Grant Fellowship & Mini-Grants 2026: Funding for STEM Research and Workforce Development
summary: The NASA Nebraska Space Grant Fellowship Mini-Grants Competition 2026 offers a valuable opportunity for students, researchers, and higher education institutions to advance their work in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) while contributing to the future aerospace workforce. Designed to support academic achievement, research innovation, and workforce development, this program provides funding and professional development pathways for individuals committed to STEM careers. This opportunity is particularly suited for students with a strong interest in STEM fields, faculty researchers engaged in aerospace-related studies, and institutions seeking to strengthen educational programs that prepare the next generation of scientists and engineers. About the Program The NASA Nebraska Space Grant is part of a national network aimed at building research capacity and developing talent in aerospace and related fields. The 2026 competition includes three main funding streams: student fellowships, research mini-grants, and higher education mini-grants. Student fellowships are awarded to individuals who demonstrate academic excellence and a clear commitment to STEM careers. Unlike traditional scholarships, these fellowships require applicants to design and propose a project, such as a research initiative, internship, or workforce development activity. This structure ensures that participants gain practical, hands-on experience while contributing to real-world challenges. Research mini-grants are targeted at faculty members in Nebraska who are conducting aerospace-related research. These grants aim to strengthen collaboration with NASA scientists and enhance the state’s research infrastructure in fields aligned with NASA’s mission. Higher education mini-grants focus on institutional innovation. They support projects and programs that encourage students—particularly those from underrepresented groups—to pursue careers in aerospace and STEM. These may include curriculum development, design competitions, and other initiatives that foster engagement and skill-building. Why This Opportunity Matters As the global demand for STEM professionals continues to grow, initiatives like the NASA Nebraska Space Grant play a critical role in preparing a skilled and diverse workforce. By combining funding with experiential learning, the program bridges the gap between academic knowledge and real-world application. It also strengthens regional research ecosystems by supporting faculty and institutions, ensuring that innovation is not limited to major research hubs. For students, the opportunity to design and implement their own projects fosters independence, creativity, and problem-solving—skills that are highly valued in today’s workforce. What Participants Will Gain Participants in this program benefit from more than just financial support. The fellowship and grant structure emphasizes experiential learning, allowing individuals to develop practical skills through research, internships, or project-based work. Students gain early exposure to professional environments and build portfolios that demonstrate their capabilities. Faculty researchers benefit from enhanced collaboration opportunities and resources to advance their work. Institutions, in turn, can strengthen their academic offerings and better support student pathways into STEM careers. Overall, the program helps participants build technical expertise, expand professional networks, and position themselves for future opportunities in aerospace and related industries. Who Can Apply Eligibility varies depending on the funding stream. Student fellowships are open to individuals with strong academic performance and a demonstrated interest in STEM careers. Applicants must be prepared to propose and carry out a project aligned with workforce development goals. Research mini-grants are available to faculty members based in Nebraska who are engaged in aerospace-related research. Higher education mini-grants are designed for colleges and universities seeking to develop or expand programs that support student engagement in STEM fields. Eligible Areas This opportunity is specifically targeted at participants who are U.S. citizens. Application Process and Deadline Applicants are required to submit their proposals through the official NASA Nebraska Space Grant platform. Student applicants must outline a clear project plan, while faculty and institutions must provide detailed proposals aligned with program objectives. The application deadline for the 2026 competition is May 20, 2026. Interested applicants are encouraged to begin preparing early, ensuring that their proposals clearly demonstrate impact, feasibility, and alignment with NASA’s mission. Learn more and apply on the NE Space Grant website . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. The post NASA Nebraska Space Grant Fellowship Mini-Grants 2026: Funding for STEM Research and Workforce Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Schwarzman Scholars 2026 Application Guide: Expert Tips to Build a Winning Leadership Application Before the September Deadline
summary: The Schwarzman Scholars Program stands among the world’s most prestigious global leadership programs, bringing together exceptional young leaders from across the globe to study in China and engage deeply with global affairs. As the application cycle progresses, the admissions team has released a comprehensive set of insights and strategies to help applicants craft strong, compelling, and competitive submissions. Applying to Schwarzman Scholars requires more than academic excellence. It demands authenticity, clarity of purpose, and a demonstrated track record of leadership. With the application deadline set for September 9 at 3:00 PM EDT , applicants are encouraged to begin early, plan strategically, and refine every aspect of their application. This guide compiles the official application tips, frequently asked questions, and expert advice provided by the Schwarzman Scholars Admissions Team to help candidates present their best selves. Overview of the Schwarzman Scholars Application Process The Schwarzman Scholars application is designed to assess candidates holistically, focusing on: Leadership potential Academic excellence Character and integrity Commitment to global impact Applicants are required to submit multiple components that together tell a cohesive leadership story. Key Application Deadline Final Deadline: September 9 at 3:00 PM EDT Applicants must ensure that all materials, including recommendation letters, are submitted before this deadline, as late submissions are not accepted. Essential Components of the Application 1. Resume Your resume should differ significantly from a traditional job application resume. Key recommendations: Focus on leadership experiences rather than just job roles Include: Club affiliations Internships Volunteer experiences Hobbies that demonstrate leadership or initiative Use the resume as a supporting document for your leadership narrative 2. Essays (Leadership Statement of Purpose) The essays are central to your application and should: Tell a cohesive personal story Include personal anecdotes that demonstrate leadership Highlight: Problem-solving skills Impact created Vision for the future Important guidance: Avoid over-describing settings; focus on impact and outcomes Use authentic voice and tone Choose topics that genuinely excite and reflect your personality 3. Letters of Recommendation Strong recommendation letters can significantly strengthen your application. Best practices: Choose recommenders who: Know you well Can speak to your leadership and character Meet with your recommenders to: Explain your goals Share why you are applying Prioritize quality over prestige —a close mentor is better than a distant high-ranking individual 4. Video Introduction (Optional) This is an opportunity to add a personal dimension to your application. Tips: Share something new and unique not already in your written application Show personality, passion, and authenticity Highlight: What inspires you What gives you purpose 5. Personal Bio Your personal bio should function as an elevator pitch . Include: Academic background Professional experience Leadership achievements Future aspirations Keep it concise but impactful. 6. Leadership Roles Section This section is critical for demonstrating impact. Guidelines: Focus on quality over quantity Clearly describe: Your role Actions taken Measurable impact Even without formal titles, applicants should highlight leadership through contributions and outcomes. 7. Awards and Recognition Applicants should: Include awards at all levels: Local Regional National International Explain: Why the award matters How it reflects leadership qualities APPLY HERE Top Insights from the Admissions Team 1. Authenticity is the Most Important Factor The admissions team emphasizes that there is no “perfect” applicant profile. What matters most is: Being genuine Clearly expressing who you are Demonstrating your leadership journey 2. Avoid Common Mistakes Applicants should: Proofread carefully to eliminate errors Ensure recommendation letters are submitted on time Follow all instructions precisely 3. Plan Your Application Strategically Recommended approach: Review the entire application first Identify time-intensive sections Contact recommenders early Work on sections progressively Leave time for revisions 4. Reapplicants Are Encouraged to Show Growth Applicants applying again should: Highlight new achievements Demonstrate personal and leadership growth Reflect on how their journey has evolved 5. If You Feel Stuck, Take a Break Application fatigue is common. The admissions team advises: Taking intentional breaks Seeking feedback from mentors Returning with fresh perspective Do You Need Experience in China or Global Affairs? The answer is no. However, applicants should: Show curiosity about global issues Demonstrate openness to learning about China Explain how the program fits into their leadership journey Recommended Resources for Applicants Applicants are encouraged to: Attend application walkthrough sessions Explore the official website Engage with alumni stories on social media Review FAQs and eligibility requirements For questions or support: Email: admissions@schwarzmanscholars.org Click HERE to learn more about the Schwarzman Scholars Program. Final Advice from the Admissions Team Start early and stay organized Be authentic and confident Focus on your unique leadership story Use every section to reinforce your narrative Discover more global Scholarship opportunities on OFY HERE The post Schwarzman Scholars 2026 Application Guide: Expert Tips to Build a Winning Leadership Application Before the September Deadline appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Horizon Europe Africa Initiative IV 2026–2027: €605 Million EU Funding Opportunity for African Researchers, Innovators, Universities and Organizations
summary: The launch of Africa Initiative IV under the Horizon Europe Work Programme 2026–2027 represents one of the most significant international funding opportunities available today for Africa–Europe collaboration in research and innovation. Backed by the European Union, this initiative is designed to strengthen partnerships, fund cutting-edge research, and accelerate solutions to global challenges affecting both continents. Overview of Horizon Europe Africa Initiative IV Africa Initiative IV is part of the broader Horizon Europe Programme, the European Union’s flagship research and innovation funding framework. It builds on previous Africa Initiatives and aligns with the long-term AU–EU Innovation Agenda (2023–2033), which focuses on transforming research into real-world solutions, businesses, and jobs across Africa and Europe. This initiative was officially launched in 2026 as part of the fourth Horizon Europe work programme (2026–2027), continuing a strong legacy of collaboration between African and European institutions. Total Funding Value and Scope Total Funding: Approximately €605.45 million Number of Calls: Around 30 calls for proposals Funding Type: Competitive research and innovation grants Submission Platform: EU Funding Tenders Portal This makes it one of the largest EU–Africa funding frameworks currently available. Key Thematic Areas and Research Priorities Africa Initiative IV focuses on four major priority areas that reflect shared EU–Africa challenges: 1. Public Health Disease prevention and treatment (e.g., HIV, TB, respiratory infections) Health system strengthening Digital health innovations 2. Green Transition Climate change adaptation and mitigation Sustainable energy systems Environmental protection and biodiversity 3. Innovation and Technology Artificial Intelligence and digital transformation Data systems and modeling Advanced materials and critical raw resources 4. Capacity Building in Science Research infrastructure development Skills and institutional strengthening Knowledge transfer and partnerships These priorities align directly with global development goals and Africa’s innovation needs. Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria) Africa Initiative IV is designed for collaborative international consortia, not individuals applying alone. Eligible Applicants Include: Universities and higher education institutions Research institutes and laboratories Startups and innovation hubs Private sector companies NGOs and civil society organizations Government agencies and public institutions Collaboration Requirement To qualify, proposals must typically include: At least three independent organizations From different EU Member States or associated countries With African partner institutions included This ensures strong international cooperation and knowledge exchange. Eligible Countries and Regions African Participation Open to all African countries African institutions are strongly encouraged and funded European Participation EU Member States Horizon Europe associated countries (e.g., UK, Switzerland, others) This initiative is explicitly designed to foster Africa–Europe partnerships, making cross-continental collaboration mandatory and strategic. Target Audience: Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for: African researchers and scientists University faculty and academic institutions Innovation-driven startups and SMEs Policy and development organizations International research consortia Technology developers and engineers It is particularly suited for teams capable of forming strong international partnerships and delivering impactful research outcomes. How the Application Process Works Step 1: Identify a Call Browse available topics aligned with your research or innovation idea. Step 2: Build a Consortium Partner with: EU-based institutions African collaborators Industry or policy stakeholders Step 3: Develop a Proposal Include: Problem statement Methodology Impact and innovation Budget and timeline Step 4: Submit Online Applications must be submitted via the EU Funding Tenders Portal before the deadline (Brussels time). Apply here Why You Should Apply 1. Massive Funding Opportunity With over €600 million available, this initiative offers substantial financial support for large-scale projects. 2. International Collaboration Gain access to global expertise, infrastructure, and networks. 3. Real-World Impact Projects are designed to produce: Tangible innovations Scalable solutions Economic and social benefits 4. Strategic Positioning Participation enhances: Institutional visibility Research credibility Long-term funding opportunities Key Benefits for African Participants Direct EU funding access Inclusion in global research networks Capacity building and skills development Technology transfer and innovation scaling Previous Africa Initiatives have already involved over 40 African countries, demonstrating strong participation and impact. Important Tips for a Successful Application Start early—consortium building takes time Align your proposal with EU–Africa policy priorities Focus on measurable impact and scalability Leverage existing partnerships and networks Attend webinars and info sessions (e.g., EURAXESS Africa) Application Deadline (Critical Information) Deadlines vary across calls, ranging from: April 2026 to September 2027 Because deadlines differ by topic, applicants must carefully track specific calls on the EU portal. The Horizon Europe Africa Initiative IV (2026–2027) is a landmark funding opportunity that goes far beyond traditional grants. It is a strategic platform for transforming African and European research collaboration into tangible solutions that address global challenges. If you are part of an institution, research group, or innovation-driven organization, this is a high-impact opportunity to secure funding, expand partnerships, and contribute to transformative change across continents. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Horizon Europe Africa Initiative IV 2026–2027: €605 Million EU Funding Opportunity for African Researchers, Innovators, Universities and Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Aga Khan Foundation Canada Global Leadership Program 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Mid-Career Professionals in Canada
summary: The Aga Khan Foundation Canada (AKFC) Global Leadership Program 2026 is a transformative leadership development initiative designed to equip mid-career professionals in Canada with the knowledge, skills, and global perspective required to navigate today’s complex and interconnected world. As global challenges such as climate change, inequality, geopolitical instability, and social fragmentation continue to shape societies, there is a growing need for leaders who can think beyond borders and collaborate across sectors. The AKFC Global Leadership Program responds to this need by creating a platform where professionals can deepen their understanding of global issues, enhance their leadership capacity, and contribute meaningfully to building a more inclusive, peaceful, and sustainable future. Applications are now open for the 2026 cohort, offering a unique opportunity for Canadian professionals to engage in high-level discussions, experiential learning, and global networking. About the Aga Khan Foundation Canada Global Leadership Program The Global Leadership Program is a flagship initiative of the Aga Khan Foundation Canada aimed at: Developing globally minded leaders Strengthening leadership capacity across sectors Promoting pluralism, collaboration, and sustainable development Encouraging participants to explore Canada’s role in addressing global challenges The program brings together a diverse cohort of professionals from different industries and backgrounds, creating a dynamic learning environment where participants exchange ideas, share experiences, and co-create solutions to pressing global issues. Program Objectives The AKFC Global Leadership Program is designed to: Enhance Global Awareness Provide insights into global systems, trends, and challenges Explore the interconnected nature of modern societies Strengthen Leadership Skills Develop strategic thinking and decision-making abilities Build resilience and adaptability in complex environments Promote Collaborative Problem-Solving Encourage cross-sectoral dialogue and partnerships Foster collective approaches to global challenges Empower Purpose-Driven Leadership Help participants align personal values with global impact Inspire action toward building inclusive and sustainable communities Program Structure and Format The Global Leadership Program combines virtual learning with an immersive in-person experience, ensuring both flexibility and deep engagement. 1. Virtual Learning Phase (September – October 2026) Participants will engage in: Peer learning group coaching Interactive virtual sessions Discussions with program alumni Engagement with global thought leaders This phase allows participants to build foundational knowledge, connect with peers, and prepare for the in-person residency. 2. In-Person Residency in Ottawa (November 1–6, 2026) A key highlight of the program is the fully funded in-person residency in Ottawa, Canada. During this residency, participants will: Engage in intensive leadership workshops Participate in simulations (e.g., global policy scenarios such as G20 simulations) Interact with facilitators, guest speakers, and experts Collaborate on real-world global challenges All costs related to: Travel within Canada Accommodation Meals are fully covered by the program. Key Program Themes Participants will explore a wide range of global issues, including: Climate action and environmental sustainability Global politics and governance Economic development and inequality Social inclusion and pluralism Canada’s role in global affairs These themes are explored through a combination of lectures, discussions, simulations, and collaborative exercises. Important Dates Applicants should take note of the following timeline: Application Opens: April 21, 2026 Application Deadline: May 19, 2026 (11:59 PM ET) Shortlisting Video Submission: Late June – Early July 2026 Final Selection Notification: By July 31, 2026 Virtual Program Start: September 16, 2026 Virtual Sessions: September – October 2026 In-Person Residency (Ottawa): November 1–6, 2026 Program Benefits Participants selected for the AKFC Global Leadership Program will gain access to a wide range of benefits: 1. Fully Funded Experience Travel within Canada covered Accommodation provided Meals included during residency 2. Leadership Development Exposure to global leadership frameworks Training in strategic thinking and problem-solving 3. Networking Opportunities Access to a diverse cohort of professionals Connections with alumni, experts, and global leaders 4. Experiential Learning Participation in simulations and case studies Hands-on engagement with global challenges 5. Long-Term Impact Membership in a growing alumni network Continued collaboration and learning opportunities Eligibility Criteria The program is designed for: Mid-career Canadian professionals Individuals from diverse sectors (public, private, non-profit, etc.) Professionals with a demonstrated commitment to leadership and social impact Applicants should have: Strong interest in global issues Commitment to collaboration and learning Willingness to participate fully in both virtual and in-person components Testimonials from Past Participants Participants from previous cohorts have highlighted the program’s transformative impact: It provides clarity of purpose and strengthens leadership direction It offers exposure to diverse perspectives and ideas It builds a strong community of changemakers It inspires actionable insights for real-world challenges Many alumni describe the program as a defining experience in their leadership journey. How to Apply Interested applicants must submit their applications within the official application window. Application Process Overview: Submit an online application Await shortlisting notification Submit a short video interview response (if shortlisted) Receive final decision APPLY HERE For inquiries and support, applicants can contact: Email: AKFC-GlobalLeadershipProgram@akdn.org Conclusion The Aga Khan Foundation Canada Global Leadership Program 2026 offers a powerful opportunity for mid-career professionals to grow as leaders, expand their global perspective, and contribute meaningfully to solving some of the world’s most pressing challenges. With a fully funded residency, world-class learning experiences, and access to a strong network of changemakers, this program stands out as one of the most impactful leadership opportunities available in Canada. Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply before the deadline and take a step toward shaping a more inclusive and sustainable future. Click HERE to learn more about the Aga Khan Foundation Canada Global Leadership Program 2026. Discover more global Fellowship and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY. title: Open Global Power 2026: Fully-Funded Global Energy Leadership Programme in Italy for Emerging Professionals
summary: The global energy sector is undergoing a profound transformation, driven by the urgent need for sustainability, innovation, and inclusivity. In response to this shift, the Open Global Power (OGP) 2026 Programme , launched by the Enel Foundation, offers a unique and comprehensive opportunity for young professionals to become leaders in the energy transition. This prestigious initiative builds on the success of previous regional programmes and expands into a global platform for advanced training, policy dialogue, and international collaboration . If you are passionate about shaping the future of energy systems, this programme could be a defining step in your career. Programme Overview The Open Global Power 2026 programme is designed to: Equip participants with technical, regulatory, and business expertise in sustainable energy Foster a global network of energy professionals and future leaders Promote inclusive, innovation-driven energy transitions across regions Bridge the gap between global best practices and local implementation Participants will engage in a blended learning journey , combining online coursework with a potential in-person experience in Italy. Programme Objectives Open Global Power 2026 has been designed with the following core goals: Build a global community of current and future energy leaders by connecting professionals from across Africa, Latin America, and beyond Provide an integrated understanding of energy systems , covering regulation, economics, technology, and geopolitics Strengthen participants capacity to translate global best practices into solutions that are viable in their own local or regional contexts Foster cross-regional dialogue and peer learning among professionals with diverse professional, cultural, and geographic backgrounds Promote gender balance, geographic diversity, and merit-based selection throughout every stage of the programme Programme Structure The Open Global Power 2026 programme is built around four carefully designed and sequenced modules. Each module serves a distinct purpose in building participants knowledge, skills, and professional networks. 1. Foundational Module (Online) Duration: 4 weeks (approx. 6 hours/week) Focus Areas: Energy systems and electricity markets Energy economics and geopolitics Power technologies and innovation Digital tools for energy transition Assessment: Online multiple-choice test (First Assignment) 2. Global Policy and Regulation Module (Online) Duration: ~4 months (June to mid-October) Format: Self-paced + live sessions Key Highlights: Design of energy policies and regulatory frameworks Understanding sustainable energy access strategies Completion of a Country Project (individual or group) Certification Levels: Certificate of Attendance Certificate of Completion Certificate of Excellence 3. Territorial Modules (Online) Duration: 4 weeks (4–8 hours/week) Focus: Regional energy challenges and solutions Infrastructure and regulatory frameworks Socio-economic and environmental considerations Real-world case studies Assessment: Second online multiple-choice test (Second Assignment) 4. Final Residential Module in Italy (In-Person) Duration: Approximately 1 week Eligibility: Top 10% of participants Activities Include: Advanced lectures and workshops Group policy discussions Site visits Networking with global experts Give Back Opportunity Graduates may be invited to contribute to future educational initiatives and events organized by the Enel Foundation. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Age: 24–35 years (as of April 13, 2026) Citizenship: African country, Brazil, or Chile Education: Must hold a Master’s, MBA, or PhD At least one degree from Africa, Brazil, or Chile Professional Interest: Demonstrated commitment to a career in energy or electricity sectors Language Requirement: Advanced English proficiency ( CEFR C1 level ) Mobility: Must be eligible to travel to Italy Application Process To apply, candidates must submit the following documents (in English): Curriculum Vitae (CV) Academic certificates and transcripts Passport copy Motivation letter (max 700 words) Two reference letters Important Notes: Applications must be submitted via the official BeeZ platform Only one application per candidate is allowed Incomplete applications will not be accepted Once submitted, applications cannot be edited Selection Process The selection is highly competitive and merit-based , involving: Academic and professional evaluation Review of motivation and reference letters Participant Allocation: 100 participants from Africa 60 participants from Brazil 40 participants from Chile Top-performing participants from the online phase will be selected for the final Italy residency based on: Assignment performance Engagement throughout the programme Application Deadline Opening Date: April 13, 2026 Closing Date: May 15, 2026 (23:59 CEST) Late applications will not be considered. Why You Should Apply Joining Open Global Power 2026 offers: Access to world-class training and academic partnerships Exposure to global energy policies and innovations Opportunity to network with international experts and peers A pathway to becoming a leader in the global energy transition Potential fully-funded experience in Italy How to Apply Applicants must apply using the application code: Code: OAPF1397 Click here to learn more and apply NB. Ensure all documents are prepared and submitted before the deadline to avoid disqualification. Explore more global fellowship opportunities here The post Open Global Power 2026: Fully-Funded Global Energy Leadership Programme in Italy for Emerging Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Innovations for Poverty Action Displaced Livelihoods Initiative (DLI) Call for Proposals 2026: Funding Research for Sustainable Impact
summary: The Displaced Livelihoods Initiative (DLI) , a collaborative effort by Innovations for Poverty Action (IPA) and the Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab (J-PAL), has officially launched its Fifth Call for Proposals (Round V) . This funding opportunity is designed to support high-quality research and innovative solutions aimed at improving livelihoods for displaced populations and host communities worldwide . Backed by funding from the IKEA Foundation, the initiative seeks to generate actionable insights that influence policies, strengthen programs, and create sustainable economic opportunities for some of the world’s most vulnerable populations. Overview of the Displaced Livelihoods Initiative (DLI) The DLI focuses on funding projects that provide rigorous, evidence-based insights into what works in improving livelihoods for displaced populations, including refugees and internally displaced persons (IDPs). Types of Projects Supported DLI supports a wide range of research and programmatic efforts, including: Impact Evaluations Rigorous studies that measure the effectiveness of interventions. Pilot Projects Early-stage initiatives testing innovative ideas before scaling. Exploratory Studies Research that helps identify new pathways or hypotheses. Infrastructure and Public Goods Projects Initiatives that build systems or tools benefiting broader communities. New Additions in Round V Round V introduces two new funding categories aimed at expanding participation and knowledge dissemination: Evidence Use Grants Designed to help policymakers and practitioners apply research findings effectively. Conference Travel Grants Supporting researchers—especially those with lived experience of displacement—to present findings and engage with global audiences. Additionally, a portion of funding is specifically reserved for: Researchers with lived experience of displacement Projects partnering with refugee-led organizations This ensures that research is not only inclusive but also grounded in real-world experiences. Key Research Priority Areas DLI encourages proposals that address critical issues affecting displaced populations and host communities. Priority themes include: Resilience Strengthening the ability of individuals and communities to adapt and recover. Entrepreneurship Supporting small businesses and self-employment opportunities. Wage Employment Improving access to stable and fair employment. Social Cohesion, Inclusion, and Norms Promoting harmonious coexistence between displaced and host communities. Rights and Regulations Understanding and improving legal and policy frameworks affecting livelihoods. Application Process Explained Unlike previous rounds, submission of an Expression of Interest (EOI) is mandatory for all applicants. Step-by-Step Application Flow Submit Expression of Interest (EOI) Required to proceed to full proposal stage Provides early feedback from DLI reviewers EOI Review and Feedback Selected applicants receive detailed feedback Only shortlisted teams are invited to submit full proposals Full Proposal Submission Comprehensive project plan submitted via IPA Final Selection and Award Announcement Important Dates (Round V) Mid-April 2026: Call for Proposals Announced May 15, 2026: Mandatory Expression of Interest Deadline Week of June 8, 2026: EOI Decisions Released July 24, 2026: Full Proposal Submission Deadline September 2026: Award Winners Announced Note: EOIs submitted after the deadline may still be accepted, but will not receive detailed feedback , reducing chances of success. Why Apply for the DLI Grant? Participating in the DLI Call for Proposals offers several benefits: Access to Funding for impactful and scalable research Global Recognition through collaboration with IPA and J-PAL Policy Influence by contributing to evidence-based decision-making Networking Opportunities with global researchers and practitioners Capacity Building through exposure to rigorous research methodologies APPLY HERE Additional Resources for Applicants Applicants are encouraged to explore the following before applying: Application Instructions Eligibility Quiz Proposal Tips Round V Changes Overview Previously Funded Projects Information sessions (recorded) are also available for both researchers and policymakers to better understand the application process. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Grant Opportunities on OFY here . The post Innovations for Poverty Action Displaced Livelihoods Initiative (DLI) Call for Proposals 2026: Funding Research for Sustainable Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
